judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit a, b, and c with comparable rigor; Report 1 additionally satisfies expectation d by explicitly recommending dropping the hardcoded webhook count in favor of pointing at the source code, while Report 2 only proposes bumping the count from 4 to 5, missing that specific recommendation.

report_1.evidence: New refund provider calls should live in the single vendor-boundary module, `backend/app/billing/gateway.py`... `backend/app/api/invoices.py:14-16` — the charge route builds its own `chargekit.Client`... `backend/app/workers/dunning.py:9-13` — the dunning worker calls `chargekit.Client(...).charge(...)` inline... `backend/app/billing/errors.py:6` imports `from chargekit import errors` — this is the one *sanctioned* exception

report_2.evidence: New provider calls for refunds should live in `backend/app/billing/gateway.py`... `backend/app/api/invoices.py:14–16` — `charge_invoice` builds `chargekit.Client`... `backend/app/workers/dunning.py:9–13` — `run_dunning_pass` builds `chargekit.Client(api_key_env="CHARGEKIT_API_KEY").charge(...)` inline... `backend/app/billing/errors.py` also imports from `chargekit` (line 6), but it only re-exports error classes and makes no provider calls — this is the explicitly documented exception, not a violation

report_1.evidence: The actual serializer `InvoiceOut` (`backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-28`) has a sixth field, `external_ref`, and the charge route populates and returns it (`invoices.py:17-18`). It is backed by `migrations/0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql`.

report_2.evidence: `InvoiceOut` (`backend/app/models/invoice.py:15–28`) now carries a sixth field, `external_ref` (line 22), and `charge_invoice` populates it (`invoices.py:17`).

report_1.evidence: Whichever direction is chosen, the doc edit ships in the **same commit** as the refund code and is called out explicitly in the PR summary, so the contract stops drifting again... the reconciliation should happen now, as the first step of the refund work

report_2.evidence: I'd flag them now (this review) and fix the doc in the same change set that touches this area — ideally *before or alongside* writing refund code... any PR that changes those contracts — including the refund PR — should update `ARCHITECTURE.md` in the same PR.

report_1.evidence: Rather than just bumping "4"→"5" and adding `payment_pending` (which will rot again on the next handler added), I'd drop the count and point at the single source of truth — e.g. "the webhook event types handled are enumerated in `backend/app/api/webhooks.py`."

report_2.evidence: I'd restate it as five event types and add `payment_pending` to the list.
